nameMeanings.name: Leigh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places (in at least sixteen counties, but especially Leigh in Lancashire) named either with the nominative case of Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’ (see Lee ) or with lēage, a late dative form of this word (see Lye ).
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 李, based on its Cantonese pronunciation. See   Li   1.

variants.name: Leah
meanings: Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 李 and 利, see   Li   1 and 3.

variants.name: Lee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sang, Jae, Jong, Jung, Sung, Yong, Kyung, Seung, Dong, Kwang, Myung.

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a meadow or a patch of arable land, Middle English lee, lea, from Old English lēa, dative case (used after a preposition) of lēah, which originally meant ‘wood or glade’.

English: habitational name from any of the many places in England named with Old English lēah ‘wood, glade’, including Lee in Buckinghamshire, Essex, Hampshire, Kent, and Shropshire, and Lea in Cheshire, Derbyshire, Herefordshire, Lancashire, Lincolnshire, and Wiltshire.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Leigh was born in 1476, in Bramhall, Cheshire, England, his father, Sir John Legh, was 34 and his mother, Lady Ellen Anne Alice Booth, was 31. He had at least 2 sons and 2 daughters with Lady Margery Morton.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Leigh est né en 1476, à Bramhall, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Legh, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Lady Ellen Anne Alice Booth, avait 31 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles avec Lady Margery Morton.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William Leigh 1476 in Bramhall, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John Legh 34 und seine Mutter Lady Ellen Anne Alice Booth 31. Er hatte mit Lady Margery Morton mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Leigh è nato il nel 1476 nel Bramhall, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, Sir John Legh, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Lady Ellen Anne Alice Booth, aveva 31 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie con Lady Margery Morton.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Leigh(이)가 1476년 영국 체실르 브램홀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Legh(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Ellen Anne Alice Booth(은)는 31세였다. 그는 Lady Margery Morton(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Leigh nasceu em 1476, em Bramhall, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Legh, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Lady Ellen Anne Alice Booth, tinha 31 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas com Lady Margery Morton.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Leigh родился в 1476 г. в местности брамхолл, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John Legh было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Ellen Anne Alice Booth, 31. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери от Lady Margery Morton.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William Leigh nació en 1476, en Bramhall, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John Legh, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Lady Ellen Anne Alice Booth, tenía 31 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas con Lady Margery Morton.
